text: The surgeon addresses the meso-appendix, ensuring it is free from the base of the appendix. The same thread loop is then used to tie a knot at the base of the appendix. The varus needle is removed, and an extracorporeal knot is tied over both ends of the thread.

The surgeon uses a knot pusher to guide the thread into the abdominal cavity, ensuring it moves smoothly over the thread. The loop is then tightened around the base of the appendix using an instrument already positioned within the abdominal cavity.

The surgeon feeds the end of the thread to the tip of the stylet, grabs it through the side hole, and pushes it through the two-millimeter incision into the abdominal cavity. The loop is then tightened over the base of the appendix.
